content:
Modern **t-SNE** evolved from earlier **Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (SNE)**, introduced by **Geoffrey Hinton** and **Sam Roweis** (2002). SNE matched Gaussian high-dimensional affinities to Gaussian low-dimensional affinities but suffered from **crowding**—too many moderate-distance neighbors could not be placed faithfully in 2D.

**Laurens van der Maaten** and **Hinton** (2008) replaced the low-dimensional Gaussian with a **Student-t distribution** (one degree of freedom), creating **t-SNE**. Heavy tails let moderate high-D distances appear as larger low-D separations, dramatically improving visual quality on benchmarks like MNIST.

The method spread rapidly in **machine learning** and **computational biology** as a default tool for **embedding visualization**, especially after efficient **Barnes–Hut** approximations made larger datasets tractable.

In parallel, linear **PCA** (see **dimensionality-reduction--pca**) remained the workhorse for compression and preprocessing; practitioners often combined **PCA-to-50D + t-SNE** for speed. Later **UMAP** (see **manifold-learning--umap**) offered another non-linear alternative with different global-structure trade-offs.

t-SNE's legacy is a clear division of labor: **linear methods** for faithful variance-preserving projections; **t-SNE** for exploratory local-structure maps—not interchangeable roles.

timeline.label: SNE (2002)
timeline.description: Hinton and Roweis introduce Stochastic Neighbor Embedding with Gaussian kernels in both spaces.

timeline.label: t-SNE (2008)
timeline.description: van der Maaten and Hinton replace the low-D Gaussian with Student-t, solving crowding and popularizing the method.

timeline.label: Barnes–Hut acceleration
timeline.description: Approximate neighbor computations reduce O(n²) cost, enabling tens of thousands of points in practice.

timeline.label: ML embedding era
timeline.description: Word vectors, deep embeddings, and single-cell omics drive routine t-SNE plots in papers and dashboards.

timeline.label: Alternatives and hybrids
timeline.description: UMAP and PCA-preprocessed pipelines become standard comparisons when choosing visualization embeddings.
